text:
Taiwan supporters have had more signs snatched at theOlympics, this time during their medal ceremony involvingChina.

In a badminton match between Denmark and Taiwan last Friday,two fans had banners in support of Taiwan ripped out of their hands, one incident involved a member of stadium security staff.

Taiwan have sent 60 athletes to theOlympicsbut are forced to compete under the name ‘Chinese Taipei’ due to a political compromise involvingChina. As part of the agreement, Taiwan are not able to compete using its own name, flag or anthem.

China claims the island as its own territory but Taiwan’s rejects the sovereignty claim made by the Chinese Communist Party.

China and Taiwan have been governed separately since they split in 1949 following a civil war.

Lee Yang and Wang Chi-lin won Taiwan’s first gold medal on Sunday as they beat China’s Liang Weikeng and Wang Chang in the final of men’s badminton doubles.

During the medal ceremony, another small banner appeared showing the outline of Taiwan while the athletes were being announced on the podium.

The banner was briefly seen on television before it was ripped out of the person’s hand.

China flags which were held by supporters in the crowd were predominately seen throughout the ceremony.

After being made aware of fans having their signs ripped up, Taiwan’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs condemned ‘the crude and despicable means by malicious people to arbitrarily snatch the slogan ‘Taiwan go for it’ from our country’s national during the Olympic Games’.

The statement added: ‘This violent act is not only ill educated but also seriously violates the spirit of civilization represented by the Olympic Games, and it is even more contrary to the rule of law and infringes on freedom of expression.’

Francois Wu, Taiwan’s de facto ambassador to France, reported the incident to local police.

When asked about the incident on Saturday, the International Olympic Committee’s (IOC) spokesman, Mark Adams, said that the conditions of entry to Olympic venues have been made clear to spectators on each ticket.

The terms and conditions of entry allow fans to only display flags of countries and territories participating in the games and forbids any banners that display political messages.

title: Olympics fans have another sign snatched during China and Taiwan medal ceremony | Metro News
description: Taiwan supporters have had more signs snatched at the Olympics, this time during their medal ceremony involving China.
